articles.title: How Americans see China now
articles.summary: As President Trump heads to China this week, a new NPR-Chicago Council-Ipsos poll finds most Americans think U.S. tariffs have hurt both economies, and that the Iran war is bad for America.

articles.title: Trump and Xi Enter China Summit Distracted by Iran War
articles.summary: The war in Iran has cast a shadow of uncertainty on both superpowers, dimming early hopes that they could begin to address the larger issues that have frayed their relationship.

articles.title: Hegseth to Accompany Trump on Trip to China
articles.summary: Defense secretaries have previously traveled with presidents on overseas trips, to provide advice and represent the U.S. military.

articles.title: Trump heads to China for state visit. And, how the war in Iran has affected inflation
articles.summary: Trump leaves for Beijing today for a state visit with Chinese President Xi Jinping. And, a new inflation report is expected to show the war in Iran's impact on the U.S.

articles.title: Kuwait says Iran attacked an island where China is helping to build a port
articles.summary: Kuwait accused Iran of launching a failed attack earlier this month on an island where China is helping build a port. And a U.S. ambassador revealed Israel sent its Iron Dome air-defense to the United

articles.title: As Trump goes to China, what do Americans say about tariffs, Iran and world standing?
articles.summary: Most Americans see China as one of the United States' biggest rivals or adversaries, but largely as an economic threat, according to a new Chicago Council/NPR/Ipsos poll.

articles.title: About 7 million kids live in a home with a loaded and unlocked gun, a study finds
articles.summary: The new study in JAMA Network Open also finds that more parents leave their guns loaded and unlocked when they have teenagers, despite the fact that suicide risk goes up for this age group.

articles.title: How Mamdani and Hochul Are Solving New York City’s Budget Crisis
articles.summary: A generous influx of state resources from Gov. Kathy Hochul and a new tax on luxury second homes will help Mayor Zohran Mamdani balance a $125 billion budget.

articles.title: U.S. Intelligence Shows Iran Retains Substantial Missile Capabilities
articles.summary: Secret new assessments say Iran has operational access to 30 of its 33 missile sites along the Strait of Hormuz, suggesting that its military remains far stronger than President Trump has asserted.

articles.title: See How Trump Is Renovating the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool
articles.summary: A rundown of the problems at the pool and what President Trump is trying to do to fix them.

articles.title: Reflecting Pool Repairs Appear Uneven and Behind Schedule, Officials Say
articles.summary: Work on the iconic National Mall basin appears behind schedule. President Trump initially said he had picked the contractor, then said he did not know it.

articles.title: How a Hantavirus Cruise Passenger Is Spending His Quarantine in Nebraska
articles.summary: Jake Rosmarin, a travel influencer from Boston, is one of 15 passengers from the virus-stricken cruise ship being monitored at the National Quarantine Unit in Nebraska.

articles.title: The SPLC survived firebombs and death threats. Will it survive Trump 2.0?
articles.summary: The Southern Poverty Law Center is facing a serious threat from the Justice Department — and comes at a time when several former and current SPLC employees say the group is already deeply vulnerable.

articles.title: FDA commissioner resigns after tumultuous tenure
articles.summary: The resignation ends Dr. Marty Makary's management of the Food and Drug Administration, which was marked by turmoil and controversy.

articles.title: Alabama will split U.S. House primaries, after the Supreme Court's voting ruling
articles.summary: Alabama will hold a special primary election for four of its seven congressional districts, after the U.S. Supreme Court cleared the way for Alabama to use a map that had been blocked by the courts.

articles.title: Marty Makary, Trump’s F.D.A. Commissioner, Resigns After Weeks of Pressure
articles.summary: The agency’s top food official will step in to the role after Dr. Makary privately said he opposed the administration decision to approve flavored vapes.

articles.title: Israel's government is expected to collapse over ultra-Orthodox military draft
articles.summary: An ultra-Orthodox Jewish party in Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's coalition has called for parliament to be dissolved, threatening to bring down the government.

articles.title: Transportation Secretary Duffy filmed a reality show, funded by firms he regulates
articles.summary: Secretary Duffy, his wife and nine kids took a "Great American Road Trip" to celebrate America's 250th. They say no taxpayers funded the project. But a nonprofit with transport-related sponsors did.

articles.title: She spent a year using AI to do almost everything. Here's what she learned
articles.summary: Tech writer Joanna Stern used AI to read medical results, respond to texts and serve as her therapist. She says her emotional connection to it was unsettling. Her new book is I Am Not a Robot.

articles.title: Inflation jumps to its highest level since 2023. Here are 3 things costing a lot more
articles.summary: Rising gasoline prices pushed inflation to its highest level in almost three years in April.  Consumer prices were up 3.8% from a year ago.

articles.title: Israel to hold military tribunal for Palestinians accused in 2023 Hamas-led attacks
articles.summary: Israel is planning a military tribunal to prosecute several hundred Palestinians accused of perpetrating the Hamas-led attacks of Oct. 7, 2023.

articles.title: Military drills in the Philippines affect the country's most vulnerable communities
articles.summary: Military drills disrupted fishing livelihoods in the Philippines, sparking protests as locals are caught between a great power rivalry and rising economic pressures linked to the Iran war.

articles.title: Israeli lawmakers set up tribunal, allow for death penalty for October 2023 attackers
articles.summary: The measure passed 93-0 in the 120-seat Knesset, or parliament, reflecting widespread support for punishing those found responsible for what was the deadliest attack in Israel's history.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
